Hawaiʻi Coastal Ecosystem Accounting

Natural Capital Accounts for the Main Hawaiian Islands

About This Project

This website presents the results of the Hawaiʻi Coastal Ecosystem Accounting project, a Pew Marine Fellowship initiative led by the Oleson Lab at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa. The project develops natural capital accounts for the Main Hawaiian Islands (MHI) following the UN System of Environmental-Economic Accounting — Ecosystem Accounting (SEEA-EA) framework.

Natural capital accounts measure the contribution of ecosystems to human wellbeing in physical and monetary terms, providing a rigorous complement to GDP-based measures of economic activity. For Hawaiʻi, these accounts document the state and trend of coastal and terrestrial ecosystems across traditional land and sea divisions (moku) and quantify the value of ecosystem services — including fisheries — flowing from those ecosystems to society.

Accounts

Account Description
Extents & Conditions Ecosystem type areas and biophysical condition indicators by moku (2013, 2016, 2019)
Fisheries Commercial and non-commercial exchange values by species group, county, and island (1997–2022)
Recreation Non-market values for marine recreational activities

Data & Reproducibility

All data processing is implemented as a reproducible pipeline using the targets R package. Raw data, pipeline code, and processed outputs are version-controlled. To reproduce any result:

# 1. Restore R environment
renv::restore()

# 2. Run the full pipeline
targets::tar_make()

# 3. Render this website
quarto::quarto_render("website")

Source code is available in the project repository. Raw data sources are documented on each account page.
